reviewNotes: Comprehensive, well-structured contract that fully covers the Sprint 4 scope (Cross-Account Search, feature 3.6). All 25 criteria are testable with clear test steps and pass/fail thresholds.

criteriaFeedback.notes: Good smoke test. Tests basic command existence with mocked multi-account data.
Sensible UX guardrail. Requiring at least one filter prevents accidental full-mailbox dumps. Test steps are clear.
Case-insensitive substring match is the right default. Test steps explicitly check case-insensitivity — good.
Mirrors C-083 structure for subject. Consistent filter behavior.
Body content search. Key to verify this works via post-filtering or AppleScript whose clause.
Good that test steps verify --since and --before independently as well as combined. Date format (YYYY-MM-DD) is implied — generator should use ISO 8601.
Conjunctive (AND) logic is the correct semantic for combined filters. Test steps are clear.
Core cross-account behavior. Verifying the 'account' field in each result is essential for the UX promise of this sprint.
Single-account scoping. Straightforward and testable.
Mailbox scoping. Test step to verify AppleScript targets the correct mailbox is a good implementation check.
Mailbox field in results complements C-088's account field — together they provide full location context.
Default of 25 matches the product spec and Sprint 3's messages list. Test steps check both explicit --limit and default behavior.
Rich table with account/mailbox/date/from/subject/ID columns. Appropriate for cross-account search where location context matters.
JSON schema with all required fields including read/flagged booleans. Validates parseable output for automation consumers.
Newest-first sort interleaved across accounts. Important for UX — users want the most recent match regardless of which account it came from.
N+1 osascript call budget (account list + one per account) is the right batching target. Testable by inspecting mock call count.
Empty results as success (exit 0) not error. Good UX — 'no matches' is informational, not an error condition.
Error handling for Mail.app not running. Consistent with Sprint 1-3 error handling patterns.
stdout/stderr convention. Important for piping and automation. Consistent with prior sprints.
Help text listing all options. Enumerates every flag that should appear — testable via string matching.
Verifies 'search' appears alongside 'list' and 'show' in parent help. Lightweight regression check.
Meta-criterion requiring 12+ distinct test cases. Good minimum — covers each filter, scoping option, limit, output format, empty results, and error case.
Full suite green including Sprint 1-3 tests. Critical regression gate.
Architecture compliance: build/parse/fetch/render pattern. Keeps the codebase consistent and maintainable.
Explicit regression check for prior sprint commands. Overlaps with C-103 but provides a direct CLI-level verification.

coverageAssessment.notes: All features from Sprint 4 plan and product spec section 3.6 are fully covered. The 25 criteria span command existence, all five filter types, filter combination logic, cross-account and scoped search, result formatting (table + JSON), batching performance, edge cases (empty results, Mail.app down), help text, test coverage, architecture compliance, and regression. Out-of-scope list is well-defined and correctly defers compose/reply/forward, mark/move/delete, doctor, completions, and integration tests to later sprints.

regressionCheck.notes: All prior sprints listed. C-103 and C-105 provide both test-suite-level and CLI-level regression coverage.
